diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: hereditary spherocytosis, pseudohyperkalemia due to mechanical trauma during venipuncture, pseudohyperkalemia due to prolonged use of tourniquet or fist clenching during venipuncture, pseudohyperkalemia due to marked leukocytosis or thrombocytosis
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with hyperkalemia without symptoms, and initial tests ruled out common causes. A marked difference between serum and plasma potassium levels indicated pseudohyperkalemia. Further testing showed excessive potassium release from red blood cells at lower temperatures (4°C and 25°C) but not at 37°C, consistent with familial pseudohyperkalemia. The patient's father showed similar results, supporting a familial pattern. No hemolysis or hematological abnormalities were found, ruling out other causes like hereditary spherocytosis.
